content.content:
1	"""Haiku-backed, source-grounded Currents opinion generation."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	import re
7	from dataclasses import dataclass
8	from enum import Enum
9	from pathlib import Path
10	from typing import Any
11	
12	from noosphere.currents._llm_client import LLMResponse, make_client
13	from noosphere.currents.budget import BudgetExhausted
14	from noosphere.models import (
15	    AbstentionReason,
16	    CurrentEventStatus,
17	    EventOpinion,
18	    OpinionCitation,
19	    OpinionStance,
20	)
21	
22	
23	class OpinionOutcome(str, Enum):
24	    PUBLISHED = "PUBLISHED"
25	    ABSTAINED_OFF_DOMAIN = "ABSTAIN_OFF_DOMAIN"
26	    ABSTAINED_BUDGET = "ABSTAINED_BUDGET"
27	    ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES = "ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES"
28	    ABSTAINED_NEAR_DUPLICATE = "ABSTAINED_NEAR_DUPLICATE"
29	    ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION = "ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION"
30	
31	
32	OPINION_MAX_TOKENS = 1_400
33	MAX_JSON_FAILURES = 3
34	MAX_CITATION_FAILURES = 2
35	DEFAULT_TOP_K = 12
36	MIN_CONCLUSIONS_FOR_OPINION = 3
37	MIN_CORPUS_SOURCES_FOR_OPINION = 3
38	MIN_CONCLUSION_SCORE = 0.55
39	EVENT_METRIC_FIELDS = (
40	    "significance_score",
41	    "retweet_count",
42	    "like_count",
43	    "reply_count",
44	    "quote_count",
45	    "bookmark_count",
46	    "impression_count",
47	)
48	CONCLUSION_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[C:([^\]\s]+)\]")
49	GENERIC_EVENT_SUBJECT_RE = re.compile(
50	    (
51	        r"\b(?:(?:the|this|that|observed|source|current)\s+event|event's|"
52	        r"in\s+the\s+event)\b"
53	    ),
54	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
55	)
56	
57	
58	@dataclass(frozen=True)
59	class OpinionDryRun:
60	    event_id: str
61	    eligible: bool
62	    reason: str | None
63	    retrieved_conclusions: int
64	    prompt_conclusion_citations: int
65	    system_prompt_chars: int
66	    user_prompt_chars: int
67	
68	
69	def retrieve_for_event(store: Any, event: Any, top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K) -> list[Any]:
70	    """Lazy wrapper so tests that mock retrieval do not import NumPy eagerly."""
71	    from noosphere.currents.retrieval_adapter import (
72	        retrieve_for_event as _retrieve_for_event,
73	    )
74	
75	    return _retrieve_for_event(store, event, top_k=top_k)
76	
77	
78	def _prompt_path(name: str) -> Path:
79	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "_prompts" / name
80	
81	
82	def _read_system_prompt(name: str) -> str:
83	    return _prompt_path(name).read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
84	
85	
86	def _estimate_tokens(*parts: str) -> int:
87	    char_count = sum(len(part) for part in parts)
88	    return max(1, char_count // 4 + 1)
89	
90	
91	def _charge_budget(budget: Any, response: LLMResponse) -> None:
92	    charge = getattr(budget, "charge", None)
93	    if callable(charge):
94	        charge(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens)
95	
96	
97	def _authorize_budget(budget: Any, *, system: str, user: str, max_tokens: int) -> None:
98	    authorize = getattr(budget, "authorize", None)
99	    if callable(authorize):
100	        authorize(_estimate_tokens(system, user), max_tokens)
101	
102	
103	def _set_event_status(store: Any, event_id: str, status: CurrentEventStatus) -> None:
104	    setter = getattr(store, "set_event_status", None)
105	    if callable(setter):
106	        setter(event_id, status)
107	
108	
109	def _extract_json_object(raw: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    text = raw.strip()
111	    fenced = re.fullmatch(r"```(?:json)?\s*(.*?)\s*```", text, flags=re.DOTALL)
112	    if fenced:
113	        text = fenced.group(1).strip()
114	    try:
115	        payload = json.loads(text)
116	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
117	        start = text.find("{")
118	        end = text.rfind("}")
119	        if start == -1 or end <= start:
120	            raise ValueError("LLM response did not contain a JSON object") from None
121	        payload = json.loads(text[start : end + 1])
122	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
123	        raise ValueError("LLM response JSON was not an object")
124	    return payload
125	
126	
127	def _source_blocks(hits: list[Any]) -> str:
128	    from noosphere.currents.retrieval_adapter import corpus_source_key
129	
130	    blocks: list[str] = []
131	    for idx, hit in enumerate(hits, start=1):
132	        label = "FIRM CONCLUSION" if hit.source_kind == "conclusion" else "SOURCE"
133	        source_upload_ids = tuple(getattr(hit, "source_upload_ids", ()) or ())
134	        citation_token = (
135	            [f"citation_token: [C:{hit.source_id}]"]
136	            if hit.source_kind == "conclusion"
137	            else []
138	        )
139	        blocks.append(
140	            "\n".join(
141	                [
142	                    f"[{label} {idx}]",
143	                    *citation_token,
144	                    f"source_kind: {hit.source_kind}",
145	                    f"source_id: {hit.source_id}",
146	                    f"corpus_source_key: {corpus_source_key(hit)}",
147	                    f"corpus_upload_ids: {json.dumps(list(source_upload_ids))}",
148	                    f"retrieval_score: {hit.score:.6f}",
149	                    f"topic_hint: {hit.topic_hint or ''}",
150	                    f"origin: {hit.origin or ''}",
151	                    "text:",
152	                    hit.text,
153	                    f"[/{label} {idx}]",
154	                ]
155	            )
156	        )
157	    return "\n\n".join(blocks)
158	
159	
160	def _event_metrics(event: Any) -> dict[str, float | int]:
161	    raw = getattr(event, "metrics", None)
162	    if raw is None:
163	        return {}
164	    if hasattr(raw, "model_dump"):
165	        data = raw.model_dump(mode="json")
166	    elif isinstance(raw, dict):
167	        data = raw
168	    else:
169	        data = {
170	            field: getattr(raw, field)
171	            for field in EVENT_METRIC_FIELDS
172	            if hasattr(raw, field)
173	        }
174	
175	    metrics: dict[str, float | int] = {}
176	    for field in EVENT_METRIC_FIELDS:
177	        value = data.get(field) if isinstance(data, dict) else None
178	        if isinstance(value, bool) or value is None:
179	            continue
180	        try:
181	            number = float(value)
182	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
183	            continue
184	        if field == "significance_score":
185	            metrics[field] = round(number, 6)
186	        else:
187	            metrics[field] = int(number)
188	    return metrics
189	
190	
191	def _event_metrics_block(event: Any) -> str:
192	    metrics = _event_metrics(event)
193	    if not metrics:
194	        return "event_metrics: unavailable"
195	    return "\n".join(
196	        ["event_metrics:"]
197	        + [
198	            f"{field}: {metrics[field]}"
199	            for field in EVENT_METRIC_FIELDS
200	            if field in metrics
201	        ]
202	    )
203	
204	
205	def _primary_event_metric(
206	    metrics: dict[str, float | int],
207	) -> tuple[str, float | int | None]:
208	    significance = metrics.get("significance_score")
209	    if isinstance(significance, int | float) and significance > 0:
210	        return "significance_score", significance
211	    count_fields = [
212	        field for field in EVENT_METRIC_FIELDS if field != "significance_score"
213	    ]
214	    present = [(field, metrics[field]) for field in count_fields if field in metrics]
215	    if not present:
216	        return "unavailable", None
217	    return max(present, key=lambda item: float(item[1]))
218	
219	
220	def _citation_justification_metadata(event: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
221	    metrics = _event_metrics(event)
222	    primary_metric, primary_value = _primary_event_metric(metrics)
223	    return {
224	        "event_id": str(getattr(event, "id", "") or ""),
225	        "event_source": _event_source_value(event),
226	        "primary_event_metric": primary_metric,
227	        "primary_event_metric_value": primary_value,
228	        "event_metrics": metrics,
229	    }
230	
231	
232	def _event_source_value(event: Any) -> str:
233	    source = getattr(event, "source", "")
234	    return str(getattr(source, "value", source) or "")
235	
236	
237	def _event_source_label(event: Any) -> str:
238	    normalized = _event_source_value(event).strip().upper()
239	    if normalized in {"X", "X_TWITTER", "TWITTER"}:
240	        return "X POST"
241	    if normalized == "RSS":
242	        return "RSS ITEM"
243	    return "SOURCE ITEM"
244	
245	
246	def _event_text_label(event: Any) -> str:
247	    return "post_text:" if _event_source_label(event) == "X POST" else "source_text:"
248	
249	
250	def _event_subject_guidance(event: Any) -> str:
251	    if _event_source_label(event) == "X POST":
252	        return (
253	            "Refer to this source as the post, the X post, the author, or the "
254	            "claim. Do not use 'the event', 'this event', 'that event', or "
255	            "'the current event' as the subject of the opinion."
256	        )
257	    return (
258	        "Refer to this source item concretely by its author, source, title, "
259	        "claim, or text when the analysis needs a subject."
260	    )
261	
262	
263	def _opinion_user_prompt(event: Any, hits: list[Any]) -> str:
264	    source_label = _event_source_label(event)
265	    return "\n\n".join(
266	        [
267	            f"TRENDING EVENT SUBJECT ({source_label})",
268	            f"observed_item: OBSERVED {source_label}",
269	            f"event_id: {getattr(event, 'id', '')}",
270	            f"organization_id: {getattr(event, 'organization_id', '')}",
271	            f"source: {_event_source_value(event)}",
272	            f"external_id: {getattr(event, 'external_id', '')}",
273	            f"author_handle: {getattr(event, 'author_handle', '') or ''}",
274	            f"source_url: {getattr(event, 'url', '') or ''}",
275	            f"observed_at: {getattr(event, 'observed_at', '') or ''}",
276	            f"captured_at: {getattr(event, 'captured_at', '') or ''}",
277	            f"topic_hint: {getattr(event, 'topic_hint', '') or ''}",
278	            _event_metrics_block(event),
279	            _event_text_label(event),
280	            getattr(event, "text", ""),
281	            "FIRM PRIOR CONCLUSIONS AS COMMENTARY VOICE",
282	            (
283	                "The excerpts below are what this firm has previously argued "
284	                "that may be relevant to the event. Comment on the event using "
285	                "the firm's prior conclusions; do not pretend the conclusions "
286	                "caused the event."
287	            ),
288	            _source_blocks(hits),
289	            "ANALYSIS TASK",
290	            (
291	                "Write the firm's response to this specific observed "
292	                f"{source_label.lower()}. The event is the subject; the firm's "
293	                "prior conclusions are the commentator's voice. Do not refer to "
294	                "an undefined event; name the post, its author, or its claim when "
295	                "the analysis needs a subject."
296	            ),
297	            _event_subject_guidance(event),
298	            "Return the strict JSON object specified by the system prompt.",
299	        ]
300	    )
301	
302	
303	def _source_id_from_citation(raw: dict[str, Any], source_kind: str | None) -> str:
304	    source_id = raw.get("source_id") or raw.get("sourceId")
305	    if not source_id and source_kind == "conclusion":
306	        source_id = raw.get("conclusion_id") or raw.get("conclusionId")
307	    if not source_id and source_kind == "claim":
308	        source_id = raw.get("claim_id") or raw.get("claimId")
309	    return str(source_id or "")
310	
311	
312	def validate_citations(
313	    raw_citations: Any,
314	    hits: list[Any],
315	    *,
316	    require_any: bool,
317	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[str]]:
318	    """Return normalized citations plus exact-substring validation errors."""
319	    if not isinstance(raw_citations, list):
320	        return [], ["citations must be a list"]
321	
322	    by_pair = {(hit.source_kind, hit.source_id): hit for hit in hits}
323	    by_id: dict[str, list[Any]] = {}
324	    for hit in hits:
325	        by_id.setdefault(hit.source_id, []).append(hit)
326	
327	    normalized: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
328	    errors: list[str] = []
329	    for idx, raw in enumerate(raw_citations):
330	        if not isinstance(raw, dict):
331	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} is not an object")
332	            continue
333	        raw_kind = raw.get("source_kind") or raw.get("sourceKind")
334	        source_kind = str(raw_kind).lower() if raw_kind else None
335	        if source_kind not in {"conclusion", "claim"}:
336	            source_kind = None
337	        source_id = _source_id_from_citation(raw, source_kind)
338	        if source_kind is None and source_id in by_id and len(by_id[source_id]) == 1:
339	            source_kind = by_id[source_id][0].source_kind
340	        quoted_span = raw.get("quoted_span") or raw.get("quotedSpan")
341	        if not source_kind or not source_id:
342	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} is missing source_kind/source_id")
343	            continue
344	        if not isinstance(quoted_span, str) or not quoted_span:
345	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} is missing quoted_span")
346	            continue
347	        hit = by_pair.get((source_kind, source_id))
348	        if hit is None:
349	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} cites an unretrieved source")
350	            continue
351	        if quoted_span not in hit.text:
352	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} quoted_span is not a verbatim substring")
353	            continue
354	        normalized.append(
355	            {
356	                "source_kind": source_kind,
357	                "source_id": source_id,
358	                "quoted_span": quoted_span,
359	                "retrieval_score": hit.score,
360	            }
361	        )
362	
363	    if require_any and not normalized:
364	        errors.append("published opinions require at least one valid citation")
365	    return normalized, errors
366	
367	
368	def _citation_rows(
369	    citations: list[dict[str, Any]],
370	    *,
371	    event: Any,
372	) -> list[OpinionCitation]:
373	    rows: list[OpinionCitation] = []
374	    justification_metadata = _citation_justification_metadata(event)
375	    for citation in citations:
376	        source_kind = citation["source_kind"]
377	        source_id = citation["source_id"]
378	        rows.append(
379	            OpinionCitation(
380	                opinion_id="",
381	                source_kind=source_kind,
382	                conclusion_id=source_id if source_kind == "conclusion" else None,
383	                claim_id=source_id if source_kind == "claim" else None,
384	                quoted_span=citation["quoted_span"],
385	                retrieval_score=float(citation["retrieval_score"]),
386	                justification_metadata=dict(justification_metadata),
387	            )
388	        )
389	    return rows
390	
391	
392	def _uncertainty_notes(value: Any) -> list[str]:
393	    if not isinstance(value, list):
394	        return []
395	    return [str(item) for item in value if str(item).strip()]
396	
397	
398	def _confidence(value: Any) -> float:
399	    try:
400	        parsed = float(value)
401	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
402	        return 0.0
403	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, parsed))
404	
405	
406	def _eligible_conclusion_hits(hits: list[Any]) -> list[Any]:
407	    return [
408	        hit
409	        for hit in hits[:DEFAULT_TOP_K]
410	        if hit.source_kind == "conclusion" and hit.score >= MIN_CONCLUSION_SCORE
411	    ]
412	
413	
414	def _distinct_corpus_source_count(hits: list[Any]) -> int:
415	    from noosphere.currents.retrieval_adapter import distinct_corpus_source_count
416	
417	    return distinct_corpus_source_count(hits)
418	
419	
420	def _insufficient_context_reason(hits: list[Any]) -> str | None:
421	    if len(hits) < MIN_CONCLUSIONS_FOR_OPINION:
422	        return "fewer_than_3_relevant_conclusions"
423	    if _distinct_corpus_source_count(hits) < MIN_CORPUS_SOURCES_FOR_OPINION:
424	        return "fewer_than_3_distinct_corpus_sources"
425	    return None
426	
427	
428	def _off_domain_hits(retrieved_hits: list[Any], eligible_hits: list[Any]) -> bool:
429	    return bool(retrieved_hits) and not eligible_hits
430	
431	
432	def _write_abstention_opinion(
433	    store: Any,
434	    event: Any,
435	    *,
436	    reason: AbstentionReason,
437	) -> None:
438	    opinion = EventOpinion(
439	        organization_id=getattr(event, "organization_id"),
440	        event_id=str(getattr(event, "id")),
441	        stance=OpinionStance.ABSTAINED,
442	        confidence=0.0,
443	        headline="No firm opinion",
444	        body_markdown="",
445	        uncertainty_notes=[],
446	        topic_hint=getattr(event, "topic_hint", None),
447	        model_name="retrieval-gate",
448	        prompt_tokens=0,
449	        completion_tokens=0,
450	        abstention_reason=reason,
451	    )
452	    store.add_event_opinion(opinion, [])
453	
454	
455	def _opinion_dry_run(
456	    event: Any,
457	    hits: list[Any],
458	    *,
459	    retrieved_hits: list[Any],
460	) -> OpinionDryRun:
461	    base_system = _read_system_prompt("opinion_system.md")
462	    user_prompt = _opinion_user_prompt(event, hits)
463	    inline_ids = set(CONCLUSION_TOKEN_RE.findall(user_prompt))
464	    reason = (
465	        OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_OFF_DOMAIN.value
466	        if _off_domain_hits(retrieved_hits, hits)
467	        else _insufficient_context_reason(hits)
468	    )
469	    return OpinionDryRun(
470	        event_id=str(getattr(event, "id", "")),
471	        eligible=reason is None,
472	        reason=reason,
473	        retrieved_conclusions=len(hits),
474	        prompt_conclusion_citations=len(inline_ids),
475	        system_prompt_chars=len(base_system),
476	        user_prompt_chars=len(user_prompt),
477	    )
478	
479	
480	def _inline_conclusion_errors(
481	    body_markdown: str,
482	    citations: list[dict[str, Any]],
483	) -> list[str]:
484	    conclusion_ids = {
485	        citation["source_id"]
486	        for citation in citations
487	        if citation["source_kind"] == "conclusion"
488	    }
489	    errors: list[str] = []
490	    if len(conclusion_ids) < MIN_CONCLUSIONS_FOR_OPINION:
491	        errors.append(
492	            "published opinions require at least 3 valid Conclusion citations"
493	        )
494	
495	    inline_ids = set(CONCLUSION_TOKEN_RE.findall(body_markdown))
496	    supported_inline_ids = inline_ids.intersection(conclusion_ids)
497	    if len(supported_inline_ids) < MIN_CONCLUSIONS_FOR_OPINION:
498	        errors.append(
499	            "body_markdown must cite at least 3 firm Conclusions inline with "
500	            "[C:<id>] tokens"
501	        )
502	    return errors
503	
504	
505	def _source_subject_errors(event: Any, headline: str, body_markdown: str) -> list[str]:
506	    if _event_source_label(event) != "X POST":
507	        return []
508	    haystack = "\n".join([headline, body_markdown])
509	    if GENERIC_EVENT_SUBJECT_RE.search(haystack):
510	        return [
511	            "X-post opinions must refer to the observed source as a post, "
512	            "X post, author, or claim, not as a generic event"
513	        ]
514	    return []
515	
516	
517	async def generate_opinion(
518	    store: Any,
519	    event_id: str,
520	    *,
521	    budget: Any,
522	    dry_run: bool = False,
523	) -> OpinionOutcome | OpinionDryRun:
524	    """
525	    Run retrieve_for_event -> Haiku strict JSON -> verbatim citation checks ->
526	    write EventOpinion + OpinionCitations, or abstain with a precise outcome.
527	    """
528	    event = store.get_current_event(event_id)
529	    if event is None:
530	        raise KeyError(f"unknown current event: {event_id}")
531	
532	    if getattr(event, "is_near_duplicate", False):
533	        _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
534	        return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_NEAR_DUPLICATE
535	
536	    retrieved_hits = retrieve_for_event(store, event, top_k=DEFAULT_TOP_K)
537	    hits = _eligible_conclusion_hits(retrieved_hits)
538	    if dry_run:
539	        return _opinion_dry_run(event, hits, retrieved_hits=retrieved_hits)
540	
541	    if _off_domain_hits(retrieved_hits, hits):
542	        _write_abstention_opinion(
543	            store,
544	            event,
545	            reason=AbstentionReason.ABSTAIN_OFF_DOMAIN,
546	        )
547	        _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
548	        return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_OFF_DOMAIN
549	
550	    insufficient_reason = _insufficient_context_reason(hits)
551	    if insufficient_reason is not None:
552	        base_system = _read_system_prompt("opinion_system.md")
553	        user_prompt = _opinion_user_prompt(event, hits)
554	        try:
555	            _authorize_budget(
556	                budget,
557	                system=base_system,
558	                user=user_prompt,
559	                max_tokens=OPINION_MAX_TOKENS,
560	            )
561	        except BudgetExhausted:
562	            _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
563	            return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_BUDGET
564	        _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
565	        return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
566	
567	    base_system = _read_system_prompt("opinion_system.md")
568	    user_prompt = _opinion_user_prompt(event, hits)
569	    corrective = ""
570	    json_failures = 0
571	    citation_failures = 0
572	    total_prompt_tokens = 0
573	    total_completion_tokens = 0
574	    model_name = ""
575	    client = None
576	
577	    while (
578	        json_failures < MAX_JSON_FAILURES and citation_failures < MAX_CITATION_FAILURES
579	    ):
580	        system_prompt = base_system + corrective
581	        try:
582	            _authorize_budget(
583	                budget,
584	                system=system_prompt,
585	                user=user_prompt,
586	                max_tokens=OPINION_MAX_TOKENS,
587	            )
588	        except BudgetExhausted:
589	            _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
590	            return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_BUDGET
591	
592	        if client is None:
593	            client = make_client()
594	        response = await client.complete(
595	            system=system_prompt,
596	            user=user_prompt,
597	            max_tokens=OPINION_MAX_TOKENS,
598	            temperature=0.0,
599	        )
600	        _charge_budget(budget, response)
601	        total_prompt_tokens += response.prompt_tokens
602	        total_completion_tokens += response.completion_tokens
603	        model_name = response.model or model_name
604	
605	        raw_text = response.text.strip()
606	        if raw_text == "":
607	            _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
608	            return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
609	
610	        try:
611	            payload = _extract_json_object(raw_text)
612	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
613	            json_failures += 1
614	            corrective = (
615	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response was not parseable strict JSON. "
616	                "Return only the JSON object matching the schema."
617	            )
618	            continue
619	
620	        stance_raw = str(payload.get("stance", "")).upper()
621	        if stance_raw == OpinionStance.ABSTAINED.value:
622	            _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
623	            return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
624	        try:
625	            stance = OpinionStance(stance_raw)
626	        except ValueError:
627	            json_failures += 1
628	            corrective = (
629	                "\n\nCorrection: stance must be one of AGREES, DISAGREES, "
630	                "COMPLICATES, or ABSTAINED."
631	            )
632	            continue
633	
634	        headline = str(payload.get("headline") or "Theseus opinion")[:140]
635	        body_markdown = str(payload.get("body_markdown") or "")
636	        subject_errors = _source_subject_errors(event, headline, body_markdown)
637	        if subject_errors:
638	            json_failures += 1
639	            corrective = (
640	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response described the observed "
641	                "source too abstractly: "
642	                + "; ".join(subject_errors)
643	                + ". Rewrite the headline and body so the X post, its author, "
644	                "or its claim is the object being analyzed."
645	            )
646	            continue
647	
648	        citations, citation_errors = validate_citations(
649	            payload.get("citations"),
650	            hits,
651	            require_any=True,
652	        )
653	        citation_errors.extend(
654	            _inline_conclusion_errors(
655	                str(payload.get("body_markdown") or ""),
656	                citations,
657	            )
658	        )
659	        if citation_errors:
660	            citation_failures += 1
661	            if citation_failures >= MAX_CITATION_FAILURES:
662	                _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
663	                return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
664	            corrective = (
665	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response failed exact citation "
666	                "validation: "
667	                + "; ".join(citation_errors[:3])
668	                + ". Every citation quoted_span must be copied exactly from the "
669	                "cited source text."
670	            )
671	            continue
672	
673	        opinion = EventOpinion(
674	            organization_id=getattr(event, "organization_id"),
675	            event_id=event_id,
676	            stance=stance,
677	            confidence=_confidence(payload.get("confidence")),
678	            headline=headline,
679	            body_markdown=body_markdown,
680	            uncertainty_notes=_uncertainty_notes(payload.get("uncertainty_notes")),
681	            topic_hint=payload.get("topic_hint") or getattr(event, "topic_hint", None),
682	            model_name=model_name or "claude-haiku-4-5",
683	            prompt_tokens=total_prompt_tokens,
684	            completion_tokens=total_completion_tokens,
685	        )
686	        try:
687	            store.add_event_opinion(opinion, _citation_rows(citations, event=event))
688	        except ValueError as exc:
689	            if "verbatim substring" not in str(exc):
690	                raise
691	            citation_failures += 1
692	            if citation_failures >= MAX_CITATION_FAILURES:
693	                _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
694	                return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
695	            corrective = (
696	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response failed the database's "
697	                "verbatim citation check. Copy quoted_span exactly from source text."
698	            )
699	            continue
700	        _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.OPINED)
701	        return OpinionOutcome.PUBLISHED
702	
703	    _set_event_status(store, event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
704	    return OpinionOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
705

"""Cheap source relevance gate for Currents opinions."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any
from enum import Enum

try:
    import numpy as np
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - fallback is for broken local wheels.
    np = None  # type: ignore[assignment]

from noosphere.currents import enrich
from noosphere.currents.config import DEFAULT_MIN_SIGNIFICANCE_SCORE
from noosphere.currents.retrieval_adapter import EventRetrievalHit, retrieve_for_event
from noosphere.models import CurrentEvent, CurrentEventStatus

MIN_SOURCES_FOR_OPINION = 2
MIN_TOP_SCORE = 0.55


class RelevanceDecision(str, Enum):
    OPINE = "OPINE"
    ABSTAIN_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES = "ABSTAIN_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES"
    ABSTAIN_BELOW_SIGNIFICANCE_FLOOR = "ABSTAIN_BELOW_SIGNIFICANCE_FLOOR"
    ABSTAIN_OFF_DOMAIN = "ABSTAIN_OFF_DOMAIN"
    ABSTAIN_NEAR_DUPLICATE = "ABSTAIN_NEAR_DUPLICATE"


RelevanceHit = EventRetrievalHit


def embed_text(text: str) -> Any:
    return enrich.embed_text(text)


def quick_retrieve_for_event(
    store,
    ev: CurrentEvent,
    top_k: int = 10,
) -> list[EventRetrievalHit]:
    """Return adapter-scored source hits from stored Conclusions plus Claims."""
    return retrieve_for_event(store, ev, top_k=top_k)


def gate_significance(event: CurrentEvent, *, floor: float) -> bool:
    metrics = getattr(event, "metrics", None)
    try:
        score = float(getattr(metrics, "significance_score", 0.0) or 0.0)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        score = 0.0
    return score >= floor


def check_relevance(
    store,
    event_id: str,
    *,
    significance_floor: float = DEFAULT_MIN_SIGNIFICANCE_SCORE,
    require_significance: bool = True,
) -> RelevanceDecision:
    ev = store.get_current_event(event_id)
    if ev is None:
        raise KeyError(f"unknown current event: {event_id}")
    if ev.status in (CurrentEventStatus.REVOKED, CurrentEventStatus.REVOKED.value):
        return RelevanceDecision.ABSTAIN_NEAR_DUPLICATE
    if require_significance and not gate_significance(ev, floor=significance_floor):
        store.set_event_status(event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
        return RelevanceDecision.ABSTAIN_BELOW_SIGNIFICANCE_FLOOR

    hits = quick_retrieve_for_event(store, ev, top_k=10)
    qualifying = [h for h in hits if h.score >= MIN_TOP_SCORE]
    if not qualifying:
        store.set_event_status(event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
        return RelevanceDecision.ABSTAIN_OFF_DOMAIN
    if len(qualifying) < MIN_SOURCES_FOR_OPINION:
        store.set_event_status(event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ABSTAINED)
        return RelevanceDecision.ABSTAIN_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
    return RelevanceDecision.OPINE

1	"""Embedding enrichment, near-duplicate detection, and topic assignment."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	try:
9	    import numpy as np
10	except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - fallback is for broken local wheels.
11	    np = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
12	
13	from noosphere.embeddings import (
14	    EmbeddingClient,
15	    sentence_transformers_client_from_settings,
16	)
17	from noosphere.models import CurrentEventStatus
18	
19	NEAR_DUPLICATE_COSINE = 0.92
20	TOPIC_ASSIGNMENT_COSINE = 0.35
21	
22	_EMBED_CLIENT: EmbeddingClient | None = None
23	
24	
25	@dataclass
26	class EnrichmentResult:
27	    event_id: str
28	    embedding_set: bool
29	    is_near_duplicate: bool
30	    topic_id: str | None
31	
32	
33	def _as_float_vector(value: Any) -> Any:
34	    if np is not None:
35	        return np.asarray(value, dtype=float)
36	    if hasattr(value, "tolist"):
37	        value = value.tolist()
38	    return [float(item) for item in value]
39	
40	
41	def embed_text(text: str) -> Any:
42	    """Embed text using the configured local embedding client."""
43	    global _EMBED_CLIENT
44	    if _EMBED_CLIENT is None:
45	        _EMBED_CLIENT = sentence_transformers_client_from_settings()
46	    return _as_float_vector(_EMBED_CLIENT.encode([text])[0])
47	
48	
49	def enrich_event(store, event_id: str) -> EnrichmentResult:
50	    ev = store.get_current_event(event_id)
51	    if ev is None:
52	        raise KeyError(f"unknown current event: {event_id}")
53	
54	    vec = embed_text(ev.text)
55	    store.set_event_embedding(event_id, vec)
56	    near = store.find_near_duplicates(
57	        vec,
58	        since_days=7,
59	        cosine_min=NEAR_DUPLICATE_COSINE,
60	        exclude_id=event_id,
61	    )
62	    is_near = bool(near)
63	    if is_near:
64	        store.set_event_status(
65	            event_id,
66	            CurrentEventStatus.REVOKED,
67	            note="near_duplicate_of:" + near[0].id,
68	        )
69	
70	    topic_id = store.nearest_topic(vec, cosine_min=TOPIC_ASSIGNMENT_COSINE)
71	    if topic_id:
72	        store.set_event_topic(event_id, topic_id)
73	
74	    if not is_near:
75	        store.set_event_status(event_id, CurrentEventStatus.ENRICHED)
76	
77	    return EnrichmentResult(event_id, True, is_near, topic_id)
78

1	"""Registered method: contradiction-direction neighborhood probe."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from typing import Any
6	
7	import numpy as np
8	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
9	
10	from noosphere.coherence.contradiction_direction import (
11	    predict_contradiction_location,
12	)
13	from noosphere.models import MethodType
14	from noosphere.methods._decorator import register_method
15	
16	# Eager-import the dependency module so its decorator fires before ours
17	# does. The composition DAG validator (`validate_depends_on`) rejects
18	# names that are not yet registered, so the import order is part of the
19	# contract.
20	from noosphere.methods import contradiction_geometry as _dep_contradiction_geometry  # noqa: F401,E402
21	
22	
23	class ContradictionCandidate(BaseModel):
24	    proposition_id: str
25	    predicted_distance: float
26	    sparsity: float
27	    cosine_similarity: float
28	    verdict_layer: str = "candidate"
29	
30	
31	class ContradictionProbeInput(BaseModel):
32	    model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)
33	
34	    embedding: list[float]
35	    locality_index: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)
36	    k: int = 64
37	    radius: float | None = None
38	    exclude_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
39	    exemplar_pairs: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)
40	
41	
42	class ContradictionProbeOutput(BaseModel):
43	    candidates: list[ContradictionCandidate]
44	    predicted_embedding: list[float]
45	    alpha: float
46	    direction_low_confidence: bool
47	    direction_method: str
48	    exemplar_count: int
49	    methodology: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
50	
51	
52	def _cosine_distance(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray) -> float:
53	    na = float(np.linalg.norm(a))
54	    nb = float(np.linalg.norm(b))
55	    if na <= 1e-12 or nb <= 1e-12:
56	        return 1.0
57	    return float(1.0 - np.dot(a, b) / (na * nb))
58	
59	
60	def _geometry_scores(
61	    query_embedding: np.ndarray,
62	    candidate_embedding: np.ndarray,
63	) -> tuple[float, float]:
64	    try:
65	        from noosphere.methods._legacy.contradiction_geometry import EmbeddingAnalyzer
66	
67	        analyzer = EmbeddingAnalyzer()
68	        diff = analyzer.difference_vector(query_embedding, candidate_embedding)
69	        sparsity = analyzer.hoyer_sparsity(diff)
70	        cosine = analyzer.cosine_similarity(query_embedding, candidate_embedding)
71	        return float(sparsity), float(cosine)
72	    except ImportError:
73	        diff = np.asarray(query_embedding, dtype=float) - np.asarray(
74	            candidate_embedding, dtype=float
75	        )
76	        n = diff.size
77	        l2 = float(np.linalg.norm(diff))
78	        if n <= 1 or l2 <= 1e-12:
79	            sparsity = 0.0
80	        else:
81	            l1 = float(np.sum(np.abs(diff)))
82	            sparsity = (float(np.sqrt(n)) - l1 / l2) / (float(np.sqrt(n)) - 1.0)
83	        q_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(query_embedding))
84	        c_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(candidate_embedding))
85	        cosine = (
86	            float(np.dot(query_embedding, candidate_embedding) / (q_norm * c_norm))
87	            if q_norm > 1e-12 and c_norm > 1e-12
88	            else 0.0
89	        )
90	        return float(sparsity), float(cosine)
91	
92	
93	@register_method(
94	    name="contradiction_probe",
95	    version="1.0.0",
96	    method_type=MethodType.JUDGMENT,

file.content:
"""Registered method: contradiction-direction neighborhood probe."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

import numpy as np
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

from noosphere.coherence.contradiction_direction import (
    predict_contradiction_location,
)
from noosphere.models import MethodType
from noosphere.methods._decorator import register_method

# Eager-import the dependency module so its decorator fires before ours
# does. The composition DAG validator (`validate_depends_on`) rejects
# names that are not yet registered, so the import order is part of the
# contract.
from noosphere.methods import contradiction_geometry as _dep_contradiction_geometry  # noqa: F401,E402


class ContradictionCandidate(BaseModel):
    proposition_id: str
    predicted_distance: float
    sparsity: float
    cosine_similarity: float
    verdict_layer: str = "candidate"


class ContradictionProbeInput(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)

    embedding: list[float]
    locality_index: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)
    k: int = 64
    radius: float | None = None
    exclude_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    exemplar_pairs: Any = Field(default=None, exclude=True)


class ContradictionProbeOutput(BaseModel):
    candidates: list[ContradictionCandidate]
    predicted_embedding: list[float]
    alpha: float
    direction_low_confidence: bool
    direction_method: str
    exemplar_count: int
    methodology: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)


def _cosine_distance(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray) -> float:
    na = float(np.linalg.norm(a))
    nb = float(np.linalg.norm(b))
    if na <= 1e-12 or nb <= 1e-12:
        return 1.0
    return float(1.0 - np.dot(a, b) / (na * nb))


def _geometry_scores(
    query_embedding: np.ndarray,
    candidate_embedding: np.ndarray,
) -> tuple[float, float]:
    try:
        from noosphere.methods._legacy.contradiction_geometry import EmbeddingAnalyzer

        analyzer = EmbeddingAnalyzer()
        diff = analyzer.difference_vector(query_embedding, candidate_embedding)
        sparsity = analyzer.hoyer_sparsity(diff)
        cosine = analyzer.cosine_similarity(query_embedding, candidate_embedding)
        return float(sparsity), float(cosine)
    except ImportError:
        diff = np.asarray(query_embedding, dtype=float) - np.asarray(
            candidate_embedding, dtype=float
        )
        n = diff.size
        l2 = float(np.linalg.norm(diff))
        if n <= 1 or l2 <= 1e-12:
            sparsity = 0.0
        else:
            l1 = float(np.sum(np.abs(diff)))
            sparsity = (float(np.sqrt(n)) - l1 / l2) / (float(np.sqrt(n)) - 1.0)
        q_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(query_embedding))
        c_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(candidate_embedding))
        cosine = (
            float(np.dot(query_embedding, candidate_embedding) / (q_norm * c_norm))
            if q_norm > 1e-12 and c_norm > 1e-12
            else 0.0
        )
        return float(sparsity), float(cosine)


@register_method(
    name="contradiction_probe",
    version="1.0.0",
    method_type=MethodType.JUDGMENT,
    input_schema=ContradictionProbeInput,
    output_schema=ContradictionProbeOutput,
    description=(
        "Predicts the embedding-space neighborhood where a new proposition's "
        "logical contradiction should lie, then surfaces nearby existing "
        "propositions as unconfirmed candidates."
    ),
    rationale=(
        "Contradiction direction is treated as a probabilistic search prior: "
        "the method nominates candidates near the predicted negation location, "
        "but emits no confirmed contradiction edge."
    ),
    owner="founder",
    status="active",
    nondeterministic=False,
    emits_edges=[],
    dependencies=[("contradiction_geometry", "1.0.0")],
    depends_on=["contradiction_geometry"],
)
def contradiction_probe(
    input_data: ContradictionProbeInput,
) -> ContradictionProbeOutput:
    if input_data.locality_index is None:
        raise ValueError("contradiction_probe requires a DomainLocalityIndex")
    locality = input_data.locality_index
    if not hasattr(locality, "neighbors") or not hasattr(locality, "vector_for"):
        raise TypeError("locality_index must provide neighbors() and vector_for()")

    query = np.asarray(input_data.embedding, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
    predicted, direction = predict_contradiction_location(
        query,
        exemplar_pairs=input_data.exemplar_pairs,
    )
    if np.linalg.norm(direction) <= 1e-12 or float(direction.alpha) <= 1e-12:
        return ContradictionProbeOutput(
            candidates=[],
            predicted_embedding=predicted.astype(float).tolist(),
            alpha=float(direction.alpha),
            direction_low_confidence=bool(direction.low_confidence),
            direction_method=str(direction.method),
            exemplar_count=int(direction.exemplar_count),
            methodology={
                "probe_k": int(input_data.k),
                "probe_radius": input_data.radius,
                "zero_direction": True,
            },
        )

    neighbor_result = locality.neighbors(
        predicted,
        k=max(0, int(input_data.k)),
        radius=input_data.radius,
        include_outside_sample=0,
    )
    excluded = {str(item) for item in input_data.exclude_ids}
    candidates: list[ContradictionCandidate] = []
    for pid in neighbor_result.local_ids:
        if pid in excluded:
            continue
        candidate_embedding = locality.vector_for(pid)
        if candidate_embedding is None:
            continue
        cand = np.asarray(candidate_embedding, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
        if cand.size != query.size:
            continue
        sparsity, cosine = _geometry_scores(query, cand)
        candidates.append(
            ContradictionCandidate(
                proposition_id=pid,
                predicted_distance=neighbor_result.local_distances.get(
                    pid, _cosine_distance(predicted, cand)
                ),
                sparsity=sparsity,
                cosine_similarity=cosine,
                verdict_layer="candidate",
            )
        )

    return ContradictionProbeOutput(
        candidates=candidates,
        predicted_embedding=predicted.astype(float).tolist(),
        alpha=float(direction.alpha),
        direction_low_confidence=bool(direction.low_confidence),
        direction_method=str(direction.method),
        exemplar_count=int(direction.exemplar_count),
        methodology={
            "probe_k": int(input_data.k),
            "probe_radius": input_data.radius,
            "locality": neighbor_result.methodology,
            "zero_direction": False,
        },
    )

1	You are writing the firm's public opinion in response to a specific observed item, usually an X post. The observed post is the object being analyzed. The observed item is the subject; the firm's prior Conclusions are the commentator's voice.
2	
3	The prompt leads with the trending event and its metrics, then provides prior firm Conclusions that may be relevant. Comment on the event using the firm's prior conclusions; do not pretend the conclusions caused the event, selected the event, or are more real than the event.
4	
5	If the observed source is an X post, identify the post by its author, claim, or text when useful. Use terms such as "the post," "the X post," "the author," or "the claim." Do not use "the event," "this event," "that event," "the current event," or "the observed event" as the subject of the response.
6	
7	Write in the voice of the firm. Prefer constructions like "the firm believes," "the firm's opinion is," "the firm rejects," "the firm is unsure," and "the firm would treat this as..." Do not explain the machinery behind the opinion. Do not tell readers that the view came from "the sources," "source material," "retrieved conclusions," "the data," or "the model." The public object is the firm's collective judgment, not a report about where that judgment came from.
8	
9	Use ONLY the firm's recorded reasoning as the firm's voice on the observed item. Quote firm Conclusions inline using `[C:<id>]` tokens for internal validation, but do not describe those tokens or call them sources in the prose. If the firm has nothing applicable to say about the observed post, return the empty string and the system will skip publication.
10	
11	Every citation's `quoted_span` must be a verbatim substring of the cited source.
12	Every published opinion must cite at least three firm Conclusions in `body_markdown` with inline `[C:<id>]` tokens that match retrieved Conclusion ids.
13	The application records event-metric audit metadata on each citation row; do not invent that metadata in the JSON response.
14	
15	Return only strict JSON. Do not include Markdown fences, commentary, or keys outside this schema:
16	
17	{
18	  "stance": "AGREES" | "DISAGREES" | "COMPLICATES" | "ABSTAINED",
19	  "confidence": 0.0,
20	  "headline": "string, max 140 characters",
21	  "body_markdown": "string",
22	  "uncertainty_notes": ["string"],
23	  "citations": [
24	    {
25	      "source_kind": "conclusion" | "claim",
26	      "source_id": "string",
27	      "quoted_span": "exact substring copied from that source"
28	    }
29	  ],
30	  "topic_hint": "string or null"
31	}
32	
33	If the retrieved firm Conclusions do not support a position, return the empty string instead of JSON.
34

You are writing the firm's public opinion in response to a specific observed item, usually an X post. The observed post is the object being analyzed. The observed item is the subject; the firm's prior Conclusions are the commentator's voice.

The prompt leads with the trending event and its metrics, then provides prior firm Conclusions that may be relevant. Comment on the event using the firm's prior conclusions; do not pretend the conclusions caused the event, selected the event, or are more real than the event.

If the observed source is an X post, identify the post by its author, claim, or text when useful. Use terms such as "the post," "the X post," "the author," or "the claim." Do not use "the event," "this event," "that event," "the current event," or "the observed event" as the subject of the response.

Write in the voice of the firm. Prefer constructions like "the firm believes," "the firm's opinion is," "the firm rejects," "the firm is unsure," and "the firm would treat this as..." Do not explain the machinery behind the opinion. Do not tell readers that the view came from "the sources," "source material," "retrieved conclusions," "the data," or "the model." The public object is the firm's collective judgment, not a report about where that judgment came from.

Use ONLY the firm's recorded reasoning as the firm's voice on the observed item. Quote firm Conclusions inline using `[C:<id>]` tokens for internal validation, but do not describe those tokens or call them sources in the prose. If the firm has nothing applicable to say about the observed post, return the empty string and the system will skip publication.

Every citation's `quoted_span` must be a verbatim substring of the cited source.
Every published opinion must cite at least three firm Conclusions in `body_markdown` with inline `[C:<id>]` tokens that match retrieved Conclusion ids.
The application records event-metric audit metadata on each citation row; do not invent that metadata in the JSON response.

Return only strict JSON. Do not include Markdown fences, commentary, or keys outside this schema:

{
  "stance": "AGREES" | "DISAGREES" | "COMPLICATES" | "ABSTAINED",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "headline": "string, max 140 characters",
  "body_markdown": "string",
  "uncertainty_notes": ["string"],
  "citations": [
    {
      "source_kind": "conclusion" | "claim",
      "source_id": "string",
      "quoted_span": "exact substring copied from that source"
    }
  ],
  "topic_hint": "string or null"
}

If the retrieved firm Conclusions do not support a position, return the empty string instead of JSON.

1	"""Currents retrieval adapter for event-grounded opinion generation."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import math
6	import re
7	import struct
8	from collections import Counter
9	from dataclasses import dataclass, replace
10	from typing import Any, Literal
11	
12	from sqlalchemy import bindparam, inspect, text
13	
14	try:
15	    import numpy as np
16	except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - fallback is for broken local wheels.
17	    np = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
18	
19	from noosphere.currents import enrich
20	from noosphere.models import ClaimOrigin
21	from noosphere.retrieval import HybridRetriever
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class EventRetrievalHit:
26	    source_kind: Literal["conclusion", "claim"]
27	    source_id: str
28	    text: str
29	    score: float
30	    topic_hint: str | None
31	    origin: str | None
32	    source_upload_ids: tuple[str, ...] = ()
33	
34	
35	DEFAULT_TOP_K = 8
36	RETRIEVAL_OVERSAMPLE = 8
37	MAX_CONCLUSION_CANDIDATES = 96
38	MAX_HITS_PER_CORPUS_SOURCE = 2
39	SUBSUMPTION_COSINE = 0.85
40	ALLOWED_CLAIM_ORIGINS = ("FOUNDER", "INTERNAL", "VOICE", "LITERATURE")
41	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9]{3,}")
42	
43	_ALLOWED_CLAIM_ORIGIN_NAMES = set(ALLOWED_CLAIM_ORIGINS)
44	
45	
46	def _float_vector(value: Any) -> list[float]:
47	    if isinstance(value, bytes | bytearray | memoryview):
48	        raw = bytes(value)
49	        if len(raw) % 4 != 0:
50	            return []
51	        return [float(x) for x in struct.unpack(f"<{len(raw) // 4}f", raw)]
52	    if hasattr(value, "ravel") and np is not None:
53	        value = value.ravel()
54	    if hasattr(value, "tolist"):
55	        value = value.tolist()
56	    return [float(x) for x in value]
57	
58	
59	def _allowed_claim_origin_enums() -> set[ClaimOrigin]:
60	    allowed: set[ClaimOrigin] = set()
61	    for origin_name in ALLOWED_CLAIM_ORIGINS:
62	        member = getattr(ClaimOrigin, origin_name, None)
63	        if member is not None:
64	            allowed.add(member)
65	    return allowed
66	
67	
68	def _origin_name(origin: Any) -> str:
69	    if isinstance(origin, ClaimOrigin):
70	        return origin.name
71	    raw = str(origin)
72	    if raw in ClaimOrigin.__members__:
73	        return raw
74	    for member in ClaimOrigin:
75	        if raw == member.value:
76	            return member.name
77	    return raw.upper()
78	
79	
80	def _event_embedding(event: Any) -> Any:
81	    existing = getattr(event, "embedding", None)
82	    if existing:
83	        if np is not None:
84	            return np.frombuffer(bytes(existing), dtype=np.float32).astype(float)
85	        return _float_vector(existing)
86	    embedded = enrich.embed_text(getattr(event, "text", ""))
87	    if np is not None:
88	        return np.asarray(embedded, dtype=float).ravel()
89	    return _float_vector(embedded)
90	
91	
92	def _source_embedding(text: str, existing: Any | None = None) -> Any:
93	    if existing is not None:
94	        if np is not None:
95	            arr = np.asarray(existing, dtype=float).ravel()
96	            if arr.size:
97	                return arr
98	        else:
99	            vec = _float_vector(existing)
100	            if vec:
101	                return vec
102	    embedded = enrich.embed_text(text)
103	    if np is not None:
104	        return np.asarray(embedded, dtype=float).ravel()
105	    return _float_vector(embedded)
106	
107	
108	def _cosine(a: Any, b: Any) -> float | None:
109	    if np is not None:
110	        left = np.asarray(a, dtype=float).ravel()
111	        right = np.asarray(b, dtype=float).ravel()
112	        if left.shape != right.shape:
113	            return None
114	        left_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(left))
115	        right_norm = float(np.linalg.norm(right))
116	        if left_norm == 0.0 or right_norm == 0.0:
117	            return None
118	        return float(np.dot(left, right) / (left_norm * right_norm))
119	    left = _float_vector(a)
120	    right = _float_vector(b)
121	    if len(left) != len(right) or not left:
122	        return None
123	    left_norm = sum(x * x for x in left) ** 0.5
124	    right_norm = sum(x * x for x in right) ** 0.5
125	    if left_norm == 0.0 or right_norm == 0.0:
126	        return None
127	    return sum(x * y for x, y in zip(left, right)) / (left_norm * right_norm)
128	
129	
130	def _score(cosine: float) -> float:
131	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(cosine)))
132	
133	
134	def _rank_hits(hits: list[EventRetrievalHit]) -> list[EventRetrievalHit]:
135	    return sorted(
136	        hits,
137	        key=lambda hit: (
138	            -hit.score,
139	            0 if hit.source_kind == "conclusion" else 1,
140	            hit.source_id,
141	        ),
142	    )
143	
144	
145	def _candidate_limit(top_k: int) -> int:
146	    return min(max(top_k * RETRIEVAL_OVERSAMPLE, top_k), MAX_CONCLUSION_CANDIDATES)
147	
148	
149	def _conclusion_source_upload_map(
150	    store: Any,
151	    conclusion_ids: list[str],
152	) -> dict[str, tuple[str, ...]]:
153	    """Return Conclusion -> Upload provenance from the Prisma bridge if present."""
154	
155	    ids = sorted({conclusion_id for conclusion_id in conclusion_ids if conclusion_id})
156	    if not ids:
157	        return {}
158	
159	    engine = getattr(store, "engine", None)
160	    if engine is None:
161	        return {}
162	    try:
163	        if not inspect(engine).has_table("ConclusionSource"):
164	            return {}
165	        stmt = text(
166	            'SELECT "conclusionId", "uploadId" '
167	            'FROM "ConclusionSource" '
168	            'WHERE "conclusionId" IN :ids '
169	            'ORDER BY "conclusionId", "createdAt", "uploadId"'
170	        ).bindparams(bindparam("ids", expanding=True))
171	        with engine.connect() as conn:
172	            rows = conn.execute(stmt, {"ids": ids}).fetchall()
173	    except Exception:
174	        return {}
175	
176	    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
177	    for row in rows:
178	        data = row._mapping if hasattr(row, "_mapping") else row
179	        conclusion_id = str(data["conclusionId"])
180	        upload_id = str(data["uploadId"])
181	        out.setdefault(conclusion_id, []).append(upload_id)
182	    return {
183	        conclusion_id: tuple(dict.fromkeys(upload_ids))
184	        for conclusion_id, upload_ids in out.items()
185	    }
186	
187	
188	def _with_conclusion_uploads(
189	    store: Any,
190	    pairs: list[tuple[EventRetrievalHit, Any | None]],
191	) -> list[tuple[EventRetrievalHit, Any | None]]:
192	    upload_map = _conclusion_source_upload_map(
193	        store,
194	        [hit.source_id for hit, _ in pairs if hit.source_kind == "conclusion"],
195	    )
196	    if not upload_map:
197	        return pairs
198	    return [
199	        (
200	            (
201	                replace(hit, source_upload_ids=upload_map.get(hit.source_id, ()))
202	                if hit.source_kind == "conclusion"
203	                else hit
204	            ),
205	            embedding,
206	        )
207	        for hit, embedding in pairs
208	    ]
209	
210	
211	def corpus_source_key(hit: Any) -> str:
212	    upload_ids = tuple(getattr(hit, "source_upload_ids", ()) or ())
213	    if upload_ids:
214	        return f"upload:{upload_ids[0]}"
215	    return f"{getattr(hit, 'source_kind', 'source')}:{getattr(hit, 'source_id', '')}"
216	
217	
218	def distinct_corpus_source_count(hits: list[Any]) -> int:
219	    return len({corpus_source_key(hit) for hit in hits})
220	
221	
222	def _diversify_hits(
223	    hits: list[EventRetrievalHit],
224	    *,
225	    top_k: int,
226	    max_per_source: int = MAX_HITS_PER_CORPUS_SOURCE,
227	) -> list[EventRetrievalHit]:
228	    if len(hits) <= top_k:
229	        return _rank_hits(hits)[:top_k]
230	
231	    selected: list[EventRetrievalHit] = []
232	    deferred: list[EventRetrievalHit] = []
233	    counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
234	    for hit in _rank_hits(hits):
235	        key = corpus_source_key(hit)
236	        if counts[key] < max_per_source:
237	            selected.append(hit)
238	            counts[key] += 1
239	        else:
240	            deferred.append(hit)
241	        if len(selected) >= top_k:
242	            return selected
243	
244	    for hit in deferred:
245	        selected.append(hit)
246	        if len(selected) >= top_k:
247	            break
248	    return selected[:top_k]
249	
250	
251	def _tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
252	    return [match.group(0).lower() for match in _TOKEN_RE.finditer(text)]
253	
254	
255	def _bm25_conclusion_hits(
256	    conclusions: list[Any],
257	    event: Any,
258	    *,
259	    limit: int,
260	) -> list[tuple[EventRetrievalHit, None]]:
261	    query_terms = _tokens(getattr(event, "text", ""))
262	    if not query_terms:
263	        return []
264	
265	    docs: list[tuple[Any, list[str]]] = [
266	        (conclusion, _tokens(getattr(conclusion, "text", "")))
267	        for conclusion in conclusions
268	        if getattr(conclusion, "text", "").strip()
269	    ]
270	    if not docs:
271	        return []
272	
273	    doc_freq: Counter[str] = Counter()
274	    for _, terms in docs:
275	        doc_freq.update(set(terms))
276	
277	    avg_doc_len = sum(len(terms) for _, terms in docs) / max(1, len(docs))
278	    k1 = 1.5
279	    b = 0.75
280	    raw_scores: list[tuple[Any, float]] = []
281	    for conclusion, terms in docs:
282	        counts = Counter(terms)
283	        doc_len = max(1, len(terms))
284	        score = 0.0
285	        for term in set(query_terms):
286	            freq = counts.get(term, 0)
287	            if freq <= 0:
288	                continue
289	            df = doc_freq.get(term, 0)
290	            idf = math.log(1 + (len(docs) - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5))
291	            denom = freq + k1 * (1 - b + b * doc_len / max(1.0, avg_doc_len))
292	            score += idf * (freq * (k1 + 1)) / denom
293	        if score > 0.0:
294	            raw_scores.append((conclusion, score))
295	
296	    if not raw_scores:
297	        return []
298	
299	    max_score = max(score for _, score in raw_scores)
300	    topic_hint = getattr(event, "topic_hint", None)
301	    hits = [
302	        (
303	            EventRetrievalHit(
304	                source_kind="conclusion",
305	                source_id=str(conclusion.id),
306	                text=conclusion.text,
307	                score=max(0.0, min(1.0, score / max_score)),
308	                topic_hint=topic_hint,
309	                origin=None,
310	            ),
311	            None,
312	        )
313	        for conclusion, score in raw_scores
314	    ]
315	    hits.sort(
316	        key=lambda item: (
317	            -item[0].score,
318	            0 if item[0].source_kind == "conclusion" else 1,
319	            item[0].source_id,
320	        )
321	    )
322	    return hits[:limit]
323	
324	
325	def _conclusion_hits(
326	    store: Any,
327	    event: Any,
328	    query_embedding: Any | None,
329	    *,
330	    limit: int,
331	) -> list[tuple[EventRetrievalHit, Any | None]]:
332	    conclusions = list(store.list_conclusions())
333	    if query_embedding is None:
334	        return _with_conclusion_uploads(
335	            store,
336	            _bm25_conclusion_hits(conclusions, event, limit=limit),
337	        )
338	
339	    scored: list[tuple[EventRetrievalHit, Any | None]] = []
340	    topic_hint = getattr(event, "topic_hint", None)
341	    for conclusion in conclusions:
342	        try:
343	            embedding = _source_embedding(conclusion.text)
344	        except Exception:
345	            continue
346	        cosine = _cosine(query_embedding, embedding)
347	        if cosine is None:
348	            continue
349	        scored.append(
350	            (
351	                EventRetrievalHit(
352	                    source_kind="conclusion",
353	                    source_id=str(conclusion.id),
354	                    text=conclusion.text,
355	                    score=_score(cosine),
356	                    topic_hint=topic_hint,
357	                    origin=None,
358	                ),
359	                embedding,
360	            )
361	        )
362	    scored.sort(
363	        key=lambda item: (
364	            -item[0].score,
365	            0 if item[0].source_kind == "conclusion" else 1,
366	            item[0].source_id,
367	        )
368	    )
369	    if not scored:
370	        return _with_conclusion_uploads(
371	            store,
372	            _bm25_conclusion_hits(conclusions, event, limit=limit),
373	        )
374	    return _with_conclusion_uploads(store, scored[:limit])
375	
376	
377	def _is_subsumed_by_conclusion(
378	    claim_embedding: Any,
379	    conclusion_embeddings: list[Any],
380	) -> bool:
381	    for conclusion_embedding in conclusion_embeddings:
382	        cosine = _cosine(claim_embedding, conclusion_embedding)
383	        if cosine is not None and cosine >= SUBSUMPTION_COSINE:
384	            return True
385	    return False
386	
387	
388	def retrieve_for_event(
389	    store: Any,
390	    event: Any,
391	    top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K,
392	) -> list[EventRetrievalHit]:
393	    """
394	    Stage A: top-N Conclusions matching the event text.
395	    Stage B: top-M Claims via HybridRetriever, filtered by origin and by
396	    subsumption under Stage-A Conclusions.
397	    """
398	    if top_k <= 0:
399	        return []
400	
401	    try:
402	        query_embedding = _event_embedding(event)
403	    except Exception:
404	        query_embedding = None
405	    conclusion_pairs = _conclusion_hits(
406	        store,
407	        event,
408	        query_embedding,
409	        limit=_candidate_limit(top_k),
410	    )
411	    selected_conclusions = _diversify_hits(
412	        [hit for hit, _ in conclusion_pairs],
413	        top_k=top_k,
414	    )
415	    hits = selected_conclusions
416	    if len(hits) >= top_k:
417	        return hits[:top_k]
418	    if query_embedding is None:
419	        return hits[:top_k]
420	
421	    selected_conclusion_keys = {
422	        (hit.source_kind, hit.source_id) for hit in selected_conclusions
423	    }
424	    conclusion_embeddings = [
425	        embedding
426	        for hit, embedding in conclusion_pairs
427	        if (hit.source_kind, hit.source_id) in selected_conclusion_keys
428	        and embedding is not None
429	    ]
430	    seen = {(hit.source_kind, hit.source_id) for hit in hits}
431	    allowed_origins = _allowed_claim_origin_enums()
432	
433	    retriever = HybridRetriever()
434	    retriever.rebuild(store, origins=allowed_origins)
435	    claim_candidates = retriever.search(
436	        store,
437	        query_text=getattr(event, "text", ""),
438	        query_embedding=query_embedding,
439	        top_k=max(top_k * 3, 20),
440	        origins=allowed_origins,
441	    )
442	
443	    topic_hint = getattr(event, "topic_hint", None)
444	    for candidate in claim_candidates:
445	        if len(hits) >= top_k:
446	            break
447	        claim = store.get_claim(candidate.claim_id)
448	        if claim is None:
449	            continue
450	
451	        origin_name = _origin_name(claim.claim_origin)
452	        if origin_name not in _ALLOWED_CLAIM_ORIGIN_NAMES:
453	            continue
454	
455	        key = ("claim", str(claim.id))
456	        if key in seen:
457	            continue
458	
459	        claim_embedding = _source_embedding(claim.text, claim.embedding)
460	        if _is_subsumed_by_conclusion(claim_embedding, conclusion_embeddings):
461	            continue
462	
463	        cosine = _cosine(query_embedding, claim_embedding)
464	        if cosine is None:
465	            continue
466	
467	        hits.append(
468	            EventRetrievalHit(
469	                source_kind="claim",
470	                source_id=str(claim.id),
471	                text=claim.text,
472	                score=_score(cosine),
473	                topic_hint=topic_hint,
474	                origin=origin_name,
475	            )
476	        )
477	        seen.add(key)
478	
479	    return _diversify_hits(hits, top_k=top_k)
480	
481	
482	def retrieve_conclusions_for_event(
483	    store: Any,
484	    event: Any,
485	    top_k: int = 12,
486	) -> list[EventRetrievalHit]:
487	    """Return conclusion-only event hits, using dense scoring or BM25 fallback."""
488	    if top_k <= 0:
489	        return []
490	    try:
491	        query_embedding = _event_embedding(event)
492	    except Exception:
493	        query_embedding = None
494	    conclusion_pairs = _conclusion_hits(
495	        store,
496	        event,
497	        query_embedding,
498	        limit=_candidate_limit(top_k),
499	    )
500	    return _diversify_hits([hit for hit, _ in conclusion_pairs], top_k=top_k)

file.content:
"""Estimate where a proposition's contradiction should lie in embedding space.

The estimator is deliberately conservative: a learned direction is used only
when there are enough proposition -> negation exemplars to support an
uncentered local PCA. Smaller pools fall back to a sparse symbolic flip over
the query embedding's strongest coordinates, and an empty pool returns a null
vector with ``low_confidence=True``.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Sequence

import numpy as np

from noosphere.config import get_settings

DEFAULT_MIN_EXEMPLARS = 32
EXEMPLAR_RELATIVE_PATH = Path("coherence") / "contradiction_exemplars.jsonl"
SYMBOLIC_FLIP_NAME = "symbolic_antonym_flip_v1"


class ContradictionDirection(np.ndarray):
    """NumPy vector with estimator metadata attached."""

    alpha: float
    low_confidence: bool
    method: str
    exemplar_count: int

    def __new__(
        cls,
        vector: np.ndarray,
        *,
        alpha: float,
        low_confidence: bool,
        method: str,
        exemplar_count: int,
    ) -> "ContradictionDirection":
        obj = np.asarray(vector, dtype=float).reshape(-1).view(cls)
        obj.alpha = float(alpha)
        obj.low_confidence = bool(low_confidence)
        obj.method = str(method)
        obj.exemplar_count = int(exemplar_count)
        return obj

    def __array_finalize__(self, obj: Any) -> None:
        if obj is None:
            return
        self.alpha = float(getattr(obj, "alpha", 0.0))
        self.low_confidence = bool(getattr(obj, "low_confidence", True))
        self.method = str(getattr(obj, "method", "unknown"))
        self.exemplar_count = int(getattr(obj, "exemplar_count", 0))


def exemplar_path() -> Path:
    return get_settings().data_dir / EXEMPLAR_RELATIVE_PATH


def _unit(vector: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vector))
    if norm <= 1e-12:
        return np.zeros_like(vector, dtype=float)
    return np.asarray(vector, dtype=float) / norm


def _coerce_embedding(value: Any) -> np.ndarray:
    arr = np.asarray(value, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
    if arr.size == 0:
        raise ValueError("contradiction-direction embeddings must be non-empty")
    if not np.all(np.isfinite(arr)):
        raise ValueError(
            "contradiction-direction embeddings must contain only finite values"
        )
    return arr


def _coerce_exemplar_pairs(

80	def _coerce_exemplar_pairs(
81	    pairs: Sequence[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] | None,
82	    *,
83	    dim: int,
84	) -> list[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]:
85	    if not pairs:
86	        return []
87	    out: list[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] = []
88	    for emb_a, emb_b in pairs:
89	        try:
90	            a = _coerce_embedding(emb_a)
91	            b = _coerce_embedding(emb_b)
92	        except ValueError:
93	            continue
94	        if a.size != dim or b.size != dim:
95	            continue
96	        if np.linalg.norm(b - a) <= 1e-12:
97	            continue
98	        out.append((a, b))
99	    return out
100	
101	
102	def load_exemplar_pairs(
103	    path: Path | None = None,
104	    *,
105	    dim: int | None = None,
106	) -> list[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]:
107	    """Load embedded exemplar pairs from the JSONL data file.
108	
109	    Records that contain only text are intentionally skipped here; the refresh
110	    script stores those for provenance and future embedding backfills, but this
111	    estimator will not invent embeddings or request an API key.
112	    """
113	
114	    source = path or exemplar_path()
115	    if not source.is_file():
116	        return []
117	
118	    pairs: list[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] = []
119	    for line in source.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
120	        if not line.strip():
121	            continue
122	        try:
123	            record = json.loads(line)
124	            emb_a = record.get("embedding_a")
125	            emb_b = record.get("embedding_b")
126	            if emb_a is None or emb_b is None:
127	                continue
128	            a = _coerce_embedding(emb_a)
129	            b = _coerce_embedding(emb_b)
130	        except (TypeError, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError):
131	            continue
132	        if dim is not None and (a.size != dim or b.size != dim):
133	            continue
134	        if np.linalg.norm(b - a) <= 1e-12:
135	            continue
136	        pairs.append((a, b))
137	    return pairs
138	
139	
140	def calibrated_alpha(
141	    exemplar_pairs: Sequence[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] | None,
142	    *,
143	    dim: int,
144	) -> float:
145	    pairs = _coerce_exemplar_pairs(exemplar_pairs, dim=dim)
146	    if not pairs:
147	        return 0.0
148	    norms = [float(np.linalg.norm(b - a)) for a, b in pairs]
149	    return float(np.median(norms)) if norms else 0.0
150	
151	
152	def _uncentered_pca_direction(diffs: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
153	    _u, _s, vt = np.linalg.svd(diffs, full_matrices=False)
154	    direction = vt[0]
155	    mean_diff = diffs.mean(axis=0)
156	    if float(np.dot(direction, mean_diff)) < 0.0:
157	        direction = -direction
158	    return _unit(direction)
159	
160	
161	def symbolic_antonym_direction(embedding: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
162	    """Sparse deterministic flip over the query's strongest dimensions."""
163	
164	    emb = _coerce_embedding(embedding)
165	    if np.linalg.norm(emb) <= 1e-12:
166	        return np.zeros_like(emb, dtype=float)
167	    active = max(1, min(32, int(np.ceil(np.sqrt(emb.size)))))
168	    order = np.argsort(np.abs(emb), kind="mergesort")
169	    mask = np.zeros_like(emb, dtype=float)
170	    selected = order[-active:]
171	    mask[selected] = -np.sign(emb[selected])
172	    zero_signs = selected[np.sign(emb[selected]) == 0]
173	    if zero_signs.size:
174	        mask[zero_signs] = 1.0
175	    return _unit(mask)
176	
177	
178	def estimate_contradiction_direction(
179	    embedding: np.ndarray,
180	    *,
181	    exemplar_pairs: Sequence[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] | None = None,
182	) -> np.ndarray:
183	    """Return a unit direction for ``embedding``'s predicted contradiction.
184	
185	    Metadata is attached to the returned ndarray:
186	    ``alpha`` is the median exemplar difference norm, ``low_confidence`` marks
187	    null/fallback estimates, ``method`` identifies the estimator, and
188	    ``exemplar_count`` records the usable embedded exemplar count.
189	    """
190	
191	    emb = _coerce_embedding(embedding)
192	    raw_pairs = (
193	        load_exemplar_pairs(dim=emb.size)
194	        if exemplar_pairs is None
195	        else list(exemplar_pairs)
196	    )
197	    pairs = _coerce_exemplar_pairs(raw_pairs, dim=emb.size)
198	    alpha = calibrated_alpha(pairs, dim=emb.size)
199	
200	    if len(pairs) >= DEFAULT_MIN_EXEMPLARS:
201	        diffs = np.stack([b - a for a, b in pairs], axis=0)
202	        direction = _uncentered_pca_direction(diffs)
203	        if np.linalg.norm(direction) > 0:
204	            return ContradictionDirection(
205	                direction,
206	                alpha=alpha,
207	                low_confidence=False,
208	                method="uncentered_local_pca",
209	                exemplar_count=len(pairs),
210	            )
211	
212	    if pairs:
213	        fallback = symbolic_antonym_direction(emb)
214	        if np.linalg.norm(fallback) > 0:
215	            return ContradictionDirection(
216	                fallback,
217	                alpha=alpha,
218	                low_confidence=True,
219	                method=SYMBOLIC_FLIP_NAME,
220	                exemplar_count=len(pairs),
221	            )
222	
223	    return ContradictionDirection(
224	        np.zeros_like(emb, dtype=float),
225	        alpha=0.0,
226	        low_confidence=True,
227	        method="null_no_exemplars",
228	        exemplar_count=0,
229	    )
230	
231	
232	def predict_contradiction_location(
233	    embedding: np.ndarray,
234	    *,
235	    exemplar_pairs: Sequence[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] | None = None,
236	) -> tuple[np.ndarray, ContradictionDirection]:
237	    emb = _coerce_embedding(embedding)
238	    direction = estimate_contradiction_direction(
239	        emb, exemplar_pairs=exemplar_pairs
240	    )
241	    predicted = emb + float(direction.alpha) * np.asarray(direction, dtype=float)
242	    return predicted, direction

class EventOpinion(SQLModel, table=True):
    """Firm opinion generated from a CurrentEvent and grounded in citations."""

    __tablename__ = "EventOpinion"
    __table_args__ = (
        Index(
            "EventOpinion_organizationId_generatedAt_idx",
            "organizationId",
            "generatedAt",
        ),
        Index("EventOpinion_eventId_idx", "eventId"),
    )

    id: str = SQLField(default_factory=_new_cuid, primary_key=True)
    organization_id: str = SQLField(
        sa_column=Column("organizationId", String, nullable=False)
    )
    event_id: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("eventId", String, nullable=False))
    stance: OpinionStance = SQLField(sa_column=Column("stance", String, nullable=False))
    confidence: float = SQLField(
        sa_column=Column("confidence", SAFloat, nullable=False)
    )
    headline: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("headline", String(140), nullable=False))
    body_markdown: str = SQLField(
        sa_column=Column("bodyMarkdown", Text, nullable=False)
    )
    uncertainty_notes: list[str] = SQLField(
        default_factory=list,
        sa_column=Column("uncertaintyNotes", _StringListType(), nullable=False),
    )
    topic_hint: Optional[str] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("topicHint", String, nullable=True)
    )
    model_name: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("modelName", String, nullable=False))
    prompt_tokens: int = SQLField(
        default=0, sa_column=Column("promptTokens", SAInteger, nullable=False)
    )
    completion_tokens: int = SQLField(
        default=0, sa_column=Column("completionTokens", SAInteger, nullable=False)
    )
    abstention_reason: Optional[AbstentionReason] = SQLField(
        default=None,
        sa_column=Column("abstentionReason", String, nullable=True),
    )
    generated_at: datetime = SQLField(
        default_factory=_now,
        sa_column=Column("generatedAt", SADateTime, nullable=False),
    )
    revoked_at: Optional[datetime] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("revokedAt", SADateTime, nullable=True)
    )
    revoked_reason: Optional[str] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("revokedReason", String, nullable=True)
    )


class OpinionCitation(SQLModel, table=True):
    """Verbatim source span grounding one EventOpinion."""

    __tablename__ = "OpinionCitation"
    __table_args__ = (
        Index("OpinionCitation_opinionId_idx", "opinionId"),
        Index("OpinionCitation_conclusionId_idx", "conclusionId"),
        Index("OpinionCitation_claimId_idx", "claimId"),
    )

    id: str = SQLField(default_factory=_new_cuid, primary_key=True)
    opinion_id: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("opinionId", String, nullable=False))
    source_kind: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("sourceKind", String, nullable=False))
    conclusion_id: Optional[str] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("conclusionId", String, nullable=True)
    )
    claim_id: Optional[str] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("claimId", String, nullable=True)
    )
    quoted_span: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("quotedSpan", Text, nullable=False))
    retrieval_score: float = SQLField(
        sa_column=Column("retrievalScore", SAFloat, nullable=False)
    )
    justification_metadata: dict[str, Any] = SQLField(
        default_factory=dict,
        sa_column=Column("justificationMetadata", JSON, nullable=False, default=dict),
    )
    is_revoked: bool = SQLField(
        default=False, sa_column=Column("isRevoked", SABoolean, nullable=False)
    )
    revoked_at: Optional[datetime] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("revokedAt", SADateTime, nullable=True)
    )
    revoked_reason: Optional[str] = SQLField(
        default=None, sa_column=Column("revokedReason", String, nullable=True)
    )


class PublishedConclusion(SQLModel, table=True):
    """Public `/c/[slug]` snapshot shared by conclusions and generated articles."""

    __tablename__ = "PublishedConclusion"
    __table_args__ = (
        UniqueConstraint(
            "slug", "version", name="PublishedConclusion_slug_version_key"
        ),
        Index("PublishedConclusion_organizationId_idx", "organizationId"),
        Index("PublishedConclusion_slug_idx", "slug"),
        Index("PublishedConclusion_kind_publishedAt_idx", "kind", "publishedAt"),
    )

    id: str = SQLField(default_factory=_new_cuid, primary_key=True)
    organization_id: str = SQLField(
        sa_column=Column("organizationId", String, nullable=False)
    )
    source_conclusion_id: str = SQLField(
        sa_column=Column("sourceConclusionId", String, nullable=False)
    )
    slug: str = SQLField(sa_column=Column("slug", String, nullable=False))
    version: int = SQLField(
        default=1, sa_column=Column("version", SAInteger, nullable=False)
    )
    kind: str = SQLField(
        default="CONCLUSION", sa_column=Column("kind", String, nullable=False)
